reasoning: The informal statement is the reverse triangle inequality for complex numbers: ||x|-|y|| ≤ |x-y|, which is a well-known true theorem. Comparing the original and corrected formalizations: the original uses `(norm x) - (norm y)` and `norm (x - y)`, while the corrected uses `‖x‖ - ‖y‖` and `‖x-y‖`. In Mathlib, the notation `‖x‖` is literally defined as `norm x` (confirmed in Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Group/Defs.lean line 76: `notation "‖" e "‖" => norm e`). So the two forms are syntactically different but semantically identical — both express the same proposition. The outer `|·|` in both cases is the real absolute value. Since on ℂ the norm agrees with the complex absolute value, both formalizations faithfully state the reverse triangle inequality. The original is faithful.
